displayTitle: Three Things Every Inspection Photo Needs

beats.narration: Years from now an auditor may read your report cold. A gorgeous close-up that could be anywhere proves nothing — evidence needs more.

payload.title: PHOTO EVIDENCE
payload.sub: What turns an inspection photograph into objective evidence.

beats.narration: Every inspection photo needs three things, every single time: a scale reference in the frame, a location or orientation reference, and an identifier.

tiles.s: Ties it to the data report

beats.narration: Frame the close-up like this: a calibrated scale beside the indication, a datum or orientation reference, and an ID tag matching the indication log.
Shoot an overview plus a close-up, check focus before you leave, and keep the original file — the indication may be ground out an hour later.

payload.heading: Field habits that save the shot

beats.narration: So here's the rule: no scale, no location, no ID — it's just a picture. With all three, it's evidence.

payload.sub: Without them, a picture — with them, evidence.
